summarization.a: The figure shows a XPS spectrum, with the focus on the Germanium peaks related to Ge-O and Ge-Se. It includes experimental, simulated, and background data.
summarization.b: The figure shows a XPS spectrum, with the focus on the Germanium peaks related to Ge-O and Ge-Se. It includes experimental, simulated, and background data.
summarization.c: The figure shows a XPS spectrum, with the focus on the Selenium peaks related to Ge-Se. It includes experimental, simulated, and background data.
summarization.d: The figure shows a XPS spectrum, with the focus on the Selenium peaks related to Ge-Se. It includes experimental, simulated, and background data.

a.question: Can from this graph be concluded that there is more Ge-O than Ge-Se?
a.answer: a	This can not so easily be concluded as a higher peak does not necessarily mean more of a material, as the peaks can both scale differently with densities of species.

c.question: What is the peak location of the selenium peak for the Ge-Se bond?
c.answer: c	The peak location is 55 eV.

d.question: How are the electrons generated used for characterization in XPS?
d.answer: d	The electrons are generated by x-rays impinging on the surface of  a material.

b.question: What is the simulated envelope composed of?
b.answer: b	The envelope is composed of a model for the Ge-O and the Ge-Se peaks. This together with a background line will be fitted on the experimental data.
